An order prescribing a shorter time under CCP section 1005(b) has not been issued (and was not requested by the moving party at the last hearing). rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HENRIETTA WEBBER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/22/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. Notice was mailed on 3/27/23, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. 2. Please also explain why it appears that interest on the Discover Bank CD was significantly lower as of Q1 2021 and again in Q2 2021 after what appears to have been an increase in Q4 2020. Interest accrued during the first accounting period is shown as a lump sum, so it is difficult to see how or if the amounts changed monthly. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/11/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN RIGNEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1)$4,035 (including $505 paid during supplemental accounting period provided in the 2/28/23 filing) was paid to prepare the accounting, which is the responsibility of the personal representative. 2)$60,893.50 in xo fees are requested by the attorney. $117,825.21 in statutory compensation is requested but timesheets show that only $101,545.50 in ordinary services as being provided. 3) ~$18,000 was paid to Miler Starr Regalia, a law firm specializing in laws related to real property, to handle a landlord tenant issue. No prior authorization was obtained, as required under Local Rule 14.50. 4)Cash in the estate was not fully covered by FDIC during the accounting period. 5)Accounting was extended by ~6 months and included additional disbursements of ~$225k). Rather than filing an amendment and providing notice thereon, a stipulation was filed by the trust beneficiaries on, acknowledging the extended accounting and requesting that the petition be approved. However: a.The parties' approval of the calculation and distribution of cash in the stipulation does not include the extraordinary fees requested by the attorney; b.The stipulation states that the parties request that the court sign the order attached as Exhibit 1, but no order was attached to the filed stipulation. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOUISA THEADORA MARIA VAN VELZEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/08/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) A copy of the most recent remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 2) Direct notice must be given to each known heir (c/o is not permitted). See CRC 7.51(a)(1) & (2). 3) Please provide an updated list of all known heirs with their full legal names and current home or business addresses (see Probate Code section 1220(a)(3)). 4) No petition has been filed under Probate Code section 11700 to determine additional heirs not disclosed at the outset of the case. LRSF 14.35(H) & (I)(6)(a)(iii). 5) The information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(2) must be provided. 6) Please provide the current balance of the estate cash on hand, together with a proof that the cash is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is otherwise held in the form of investment(s) authorized by Probate Code section 9730. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE YANG SU CHO 2015 LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues need to be cleared: a) Need Notice of Hearing with a copy of petition to be served 30-days before the hearing. PC 17203. Notice was mailed to Kyung Ja Kim and Eun Tae Kim on 3/17/2023. Failed to meet the 30-day notice requirement. b) Written consent by the proposed succ trustee, and Notice of Hearing to be served on the nominee. c) Authority or provision of the trust under which the court can waive bond as to non-trust nominated successor trustee. d) The suspended trustee is also the beneficiary whose written waiver of bond needed. e) Proposed order to be submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID GOLDMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/15/23. (No earlier available date.) 1. A Judicial Council form must not be used for a supplement to a pleading. [CRC 7.104(b)(2).] 2. A supplement to the Spousal Property Petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed that just completes item 1 of the petition and has a copy of the vesting deed attached to it. 3. The proof of service filed on 3/20/23 shows that notice was mailed on 3/20/23, but the verification was signed on 3/10/23. Deadline is 5/4/23 for providing (1) a courtesy copy of the supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and completing item 1 of the petition and attaching a copy of the vesting deed, and (2) a courtesy copy of the proof of service of notice that includes the language regarding remote appearance procedures and is correctly verified after the notice is mailed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HUGO DE JESUS HERNANDEZ HIDALGO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAMERON REED FULLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1)The order appointing special administrator expires on 4/10/23. 2)Re: Petition for Letters a.The caption does not match the body of the petition (is petitioner requesting authority under the Independent Administration of Estates Act?). b.Item 2d is incomplete (bond). c.Item 3d is incomplete (estimated value of estate). d.Item 3h is incomplete (whether petitioner is a resident of California). e.Item 6 is incomplete (who Decedent was survived by). f.Item 7 is incomplete. 3)Form DE-147 is incomplete (address and phone number). The form must be re-filed. 4)Form DE-147S has not been filed, which must be filed in person and attached to a cover sheet. 5)Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has not been filed. 6)Proof of Publication has not been filed. 7)Proposed order has not been submitted. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/24/2023. A copy of the most recent remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MEN CHEW GEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/24/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) It appears, at first glance, that the petition is incomplete (caption is incomplete, item 6f of the petition is not the first box that applies if niblings survive, and the names and dates of death of predeceased heirs appears to be missing from item 8 of the petition) [LRSF 14.18 C. and D.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
